name: Account
description: Account mnemonic as agreed between buy and sell sides, used for clearing and allocation.
notes: Format varies by counterparty and asset class. In equities, this is typically a clearing account number. Absent in many algorithmic order flows where account is inferred from SenderCompID (49).

name: AvgPx
description: Calculated average price of all fills on this order.
notes: Weighted average of all fill prices on this order to date. Will be 0 for unfilled orders. Calculated as sum(LastPx × LastQty) / CumQty. The currency is specified by Currency (15).

name: BeginSeqNo
description: Indicates the sequence number of the first message in the range to be resent. Used in Resend Request (2) to request the counterparty retransmit a sequence of messages starting from this number.
notes: Always paired with EndSeqNo (16). A gap in incoming sequence numbers triggers a Resend Request with BeginSeqNo set to the first missing message number.

name: BeginString
description: Identifies beginning of a new message and the protocol version. Must always be the first field in any FIX message.
notes: Always tag 8, always first. The value determines which version of the protocol governs message field requirements. FIX.4.2 and FIX.4.4 are the most common in production equity trading environments today.

name: BodyLength
description: Message length in bytes, counted from the byte immediately after BodyLength's SOH delimiter to (but not including) the delimiter before CheckSum (10).
notes: Always the second field in any FIX message. Used by parsers to validate message framing. A mismatch between BodyLength and the actual byte count will cause a session-level Reject (MsgType=3).

name: CheckSum
description: Three-digit, zero-padded modulo-256 checksum of all bytes in the message up to and including the SOH before this field.
notes: Always the last field in any FIX message. The value is always exactly 3 digits (e.g. '007', '128', '255'). Calculated by summing ASCII values of all bytes before this field modulo 256. A bad checksum causes a session-level Reject.

name: ClOrdID
description: Unique identifier for an order as assigned by the buy-side (client). Must remain constant for the life of an order.
notes: The buy-side must ensure ClOrdID is unique within a trading session. Sell-sides use it to correlate Execution Reports back to the originating order. Cancel and replace requests must include the ClOrdID of the current live order.

name: Commission
description: Commission amount for the order or fill.
notes: Used with CommType (13) to fully specify the fee. For CommType=2 (percentage), express as decimal, e.g. 0.0020 for 20 bps.

name: CommType
description: Indicates the type of commission structure for the order.
notes: Used alongside Tag 12 (Commission) to fully describe the fee structure. When CommType=2 (percentage), the Commission value should be expressed as a decimal (e.g. 0.002 for 20 bps).

name: CumQty
description: Total quantity (shares/contracts) filled on this order since it was submitted.
notes: Running cumulative fill total. Starts at 0 on the first Execution Report (ExecType=New) and increases with each fill. For a fully filled order: CumQty = OrderQty (38). Related: LeavesQty (151) = OrderQty − CumQty.

name: Currency
description: Currency used for price. Uses ISO 4217 three-character currency codes.
notes: If omitted, the default currency for the security's primary exchange is assumed. Important for cross-currency trading and FX orders where the denomination may differ from the instrument's native currency.

name: EndSeqNo
description: Indicates the sequence number of the last message in the range to be resent. A value of 0 (zero) means "resend to infinity" — all messages from BeginSeqNo to the current sequence number.
notes: Always paired with BeginSeqNo (7). Setting EndSeqNo=0 requests all messages from the gap to the current point, which is the most common usage.

name: ExecID
description: Unique identifier of each Execution Report message as assigned by the sell-side.
notes: Every Execution Report must have a unique ExecID. Used as the primary key for referencing a specific execution in allocation, confirmation, and booking messages. ExecID values must never be reused within a session.

name: ExecInst
description: Free-format instructions for order handling. May contain multiple values separated by spaces.

valid_values.1: Not held — trader has discretion on timing
valid_values.2: Work — work the order with discretion
valid_values.3: Go along — participate alongside market flow

notes: Multiple values can appear in a single field separated by spaces (e.g. '1 A' means Not Held and No Cross). These are execution strategy hints passed from buy-side to sell-side and onward to the venue.

name: ExecTransType
description: Identifies the type of execution transaction. Deprecated in FIX 4.2 in favour of ExecType (150).
notes: Required in FIX 4.0 and 4.1 Execution Reports. In FIX 4.2+, replaced by ExecType (150). Still seen in legacy 4.2 implementations.

name: HandlInst
description: Instructions for order handling on the broker trading floor. Indicates whether the order is automated or requires manual intervention.
notes: Value 1 is used for the vast majority of algorithmic and DMA orders. Value 3 indicates the order requires a human trader on the sell-side. Affects how the broker is required to handle and prioritize the order.

name: SecurityIDSource
description: Identifies the class or source of the SecurityID (48) value. Tells the receiver how to interpret the security identifier.
notes: Named IDSource in FIX 4.1 and earlier; renamed SecurityIDSource in FIX 4.2. Must always accompany SecurityID (48). The most common values in US equity trading are 1 (CUSIP) and 8 (Exchange Symbol).

name: LastCapacity
description: Indicates the capacity in which the broker executed the most recent fill.
notes: Has regulatory significance in most jurisdictions. Whether the broker was acting as agent (on behalf of the client) or principal (trading its own book) affects disclosure requirements, best execution obligations, and commission treatment.

name: LastMkt
description: Market (exchange or venue) where the most recent fill was executed.
notes: Uses ISO 10383 MIC (Market Identifier Code) for the execution venue. Important for Reg NMS trade-through compliance analysis and best execution reporting. Each fill in a partial fill scenario may have a different LastMkt.

name: LastPx
description: Price at which the most recent fill occurred.
notes: Populated in Execution Reports where ExecType=F (Trade). Used with LastQty (32) to describe each individual fill. The running average across all fills is AvgPx (6). For the first fill, LastPx is also equal to AvgPx.

name: LastQty
description: Quantity bought or sold on the most recent fill.
notes: Called LastShares in FIX 4.1 and earlier; renamed LastQty in FIX 4.2. Always paired with LastPx (31). The contribution to CumQty is: new CumQty = old CumQty + LastQty.

name: MsgSeqNum
description: Integer message sequence number. Strictly sequential, starting from 1 at session initiation.
notes: Both sides maintain independent, monotonically increasing sequence number series. Gaps cause ResendRequests (MsgType=2). Sequence numbers reset to 1 on Logon when ResetSeqNumFlag (141)=Y. Out-of-order delivery causes a session-level Reject.

name: MsgType
description: Defines message type. Must be the third field in every FIX message, immediately following BeginString (8) and BodyLength (9).
notes: Lowercase letters were introduced in FIX 4.3; two-letter types in FIX 4.4. The most common types in equity trading are D (New Order Single), 8 (Execution Report), F (Cancel), G (Cancel/Replace), 9 (Cancel Reject), and the session messages 0, A, 5.

name: NewSeqNo
description: New sequence number to reset the outgoing message sequence to. In a Gap Fill Sequence Reset (GapFillFlag=Y), indicates the new expected sequence number after skipping the gap. In a Reset (GapFillFlag=N or absent), forces both sides to reset.
notes: Used in SequenceReset (MsgType=4) to jump the sequence forward, skipping messages that do not need to be retransmitted (e.g. Heartbeats). The receiver must update its expected inbound sequence number to NewSeqNo. Never used to decrease the sequence number.

name: OrderID
description: Unique identifier for an order as assigned by the sell-side (broker or exchange).
notes: Assigned by the sell-side upon receipt of a New Order Single. Echoed back in all Execution Reports for that order. Buy-side systems must store OrderID to reference the order in cancel and status requests. May be an exchange-assigned order reference number.

name: OrderQty
description: Total number of shares or contracts requested in the order.
notes: For equity orders, expressed in whole shares. For futures and options, expressed in number of contracts. The order is complete when CumQty (14) = OrderQty. In a Cancel/Replace, providing a new OrderQty modifies the order size.

name: OrdStatus
description: Identifies the current status of an order as of the time this Execution Report was generated.
notes: Represents the cumulative state of the order, not just the current transaction. OrdStatus must be read alongside ExecType (150): ExecType describes what just happened (e.g. a fill), while OrdStatus describes the order's overall state (e.g. Partially Filled).

name: OrdType
description: Order type. Determines how the order is priced and matched at the venue.
notes: Market (1) and Limit (2) are by far the most common. For Limit orders, Price (44) is required. For Stop orders, StopPx (99) is required. For Stop Limit orders, both Price (44) and StopPx (99) are required.

name: OrigClOrdID
description: The ClOrdID (11) of the most recent prior order in the order chain. Required in cancel and cancel/replace requests.
notes: Must match the ClOrdID of the most recent active order version. The pair (OrigClOrdID, ClOrdID) creates an audit chain: OrigClOrdID points to the order being cancelled, ClOrdID is the new identifier for this cancel request. In an Order Cancel Reject (9), OrigClOrdID echoes back the value from the failed cancel request.

name: PossDupFlag
description: Indicates possible retransmission of a message with the same sequence number.

valid_values.Y: Possible duplicate — message may have been previously received

notes: Set to Y when resending a message during a ResendRequest (MsgType=2) recovery sequence. The receiving application must check whether it has already processed a message with the same MsgSeqNum (34) and take appropriate action to avoid duplicate order submission.

name: Price
description: Limit price per unit of quantity. Required for Limit and Stop Limit orders.
notes: Required when OrdType (40) = 2 (Limit) or 4 (Stop Limit). Not applicable to Market orders (OrdType=1). The currency is specified by Currency (15). For bonds, price may be expressed as a percentage of par value rather than an absolute dollar amount.

name: RefSeqNum
description: Reference message sequence number (MsgSeqNum Tag 34) of the message being rejected or referenced. In Reject (3) messages, points to the specific message that caused the rejection.
notes: In a ResendRequest (MsgType=2), identifies the start of the sequence range being requested. In a Reject (MsgType=3), identifies the MsgSeqNum of the message that was rejected. Always corresponds to a MsgSeqNum (34) value from the counterparty's outbound message stream.

name: Rule80A
description: Indicates algorithm for order handling and execution under NYSE/AMEX Rule 80A. Deprecated in FIX 4.3.

valid_values.C: Prog order non-index arb for member firm
valid_values.D: Prog order index arb for member firm
valid_values.F: Short exempt trans not for member
valid_values.H: Short exempt trans principal (subset of E)
valid_values.J: Prog order index arb for individual
valid_values.M: Prog order index arb for other org
valid_values.O: Prog order non-index arb for org
valid_values.U: Prog order index arb for other member
valid_values.W: All other orders for member and org

notes: Regulatory field for NYSE/AMEX equity trading. Deprecated in FIX 4.3; replaced by more granular fields.

name: SecurityID
description: Security identifier value. The format is determined by SecurityIDSource (22).
notes: Must always be accompanied by SecurityIDSource (22) to indicate the identifier type (e.g. CUSIP, ISIN, SEDOL). Commonly used alongside Symbol (55) where the symbol provides human-readable context and SecurityID provides the machine-authoritative identifier.

name: SenderCompID
description: Assigned value used to identify the firm sending the message.
notes: Agreed upon during session setup between counterparties. Together with TargetCompID (56), SenderCompID forms the session identifier. The receiver must validate that SenderCompID in received messages matches the expected counterparty. Must swap to TargetCompID in replies.

name: SenderSubID
description: Assigned value used to identify specific message originator's sub-department within the firm. Used in conjunction with SenderCompID (49) to route messages within a firm or to a specific desk or trader. Essential in give-up workflows where messages originate from specific sub-entities.
notes: Part of the standard optional FIX session header. Critical for give-up trades, prime-brokerage, and on-behalf-of workflows where messages are routed through or on behalf of intermediaries.

name: SendingTime
description: Time of message transmission. Always expressed in UTC.
notes: Format: YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS[.sss] where milliseconds are optional but recommended. Must be in UTC regardless of the trading venue's local timezone. FIX engines validate that SendingTime is within a tolerance window (typically ±120 seconds) of the receiver's clock. The delta between SendingTime and TransactTime (60) measures processing latency.

name: Side
description: Side of the order — buy or sell. Required in all order-related messages.
notes: Values 5 (Sell Short) and 6 (Sell Short Exempt) carry regulatory significance under Reg SHO. Sell Short requires the broker to have located borrows. Sell Short Exempt is used for market-making activity exempt from locate requirements. Value 1 (Buy) and 2 (Sell) account for the vast majority of order flow.

name: Symbol
description: Ticker symbol — the common, human-understood representation of the security.
notes: The exchange ticker symbol (e.g. AAPL, MSFT, SPY). Value '[N/A]' may be used if the security is identified exclusively via SecurityID (48). For multi-leg instruments, Symbol may identify the strategy while individual legs are in repeating groups.

name: TargetCompID
description: Assigned value used to identify the firm receiving the message.
notes: The counterpart to SenderCompID (49). Agreed during session setup. The receiver validates that its own identity matches the incoming TargetCompID; a mismatch causes a session-level Reject (MsgType=3) with SessionRejectReason=9 (CompID Problem).

name: TargetSubID
description: Assigned value used to identify specific individual or unit intended to receive the message within the target firm. Used with TargetCompID (56) for intra-firm message routing. Required by many venues to route to a specific trading desk.
notes: Part of the standard optional FIX session header. Critical for give-up trades, prime-brokerage, and on-behalf-of workflows where messages are routed through or on behalf of intermediaries.

name: Text
description: Free format text string. Used for human-readable context, error descriptions, or supplemental order instructions.
notes: Commonly used in Rejects (MsgType=3, 9) to explain the rejection reason in plain language alongside the structured reason code. Not machine-parseable; counterparties should not rely on the content of Text for automated processing. See EncodedText (355) for non-ASCII characters.

name: TimeInForce
description: Specifies how long the order remains in effect. Governs when the order expires if not fully filled.

valid_values.0: Day — expires at end of trading day (default)
valid_values.1: Good Till Cancel (GTC) — rests until explicitly cancelled
valid_values.2: At the Opening (OPG) — must execute at the opening or cancel
valid_values.3: Immediate or Cancel (IOC) — execute available quantity, cancel remainder
valid_values.4: Fill or Kill (FOK) — execute entire quantity immediately or cancel all
valid_values.6: Good Till Date (GTD) — requires ExpireDate (432) or ExpireTime (126)
valid_values.7: At the Close — executes at closing price or cancels

notes: If omitted, Day (0) is assumed by most execution venues. GTD (6) requires an ExpireDate (432) or ExpireTime (126) to specify the expiry. FOK (4) and IOC (3) are common for algorithmic strategies that cannot accept partial fills or resting orders.

name: TransactTime
description: Time the transaction represented by this message occurred — when the order was submitted, the fill happened, or the cancellation was processed. Always expressed in UTC.
notes: Distinct from SendingTime (52): TransactTime is when the business event occurred; SendingTime is when the FIX message was sent. The difference between these two timestamps is the processing latency introduced by the send-side system. Both must be in UTC and formatted as YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS[.sss].

name: ValidUntilTime
description: Time until which an IOI or quote remains valid.
notes: After this time the quote or IOI should be considered expired by the recipient.

name: SettlType
description: Indicates the settlement period for the order.
notes: Named SettlmntTyp in FIX 4.1 and earlier; renamed SettlType in FIX 4.2. Default settlement depends on security type and market. US equities shifted to T+2 in 2017 and T+1 in 2024. Cash settlement (1) is used for same-day obligations.

name: SymbolSfx
description: Additional information about the security symbol to differentiate it, e.g. 'WI' for When Issued.
notes: Used in conjunction with Symbol (55) when additional qualifier is needed to uniquely identify a security.

name: ListID
description: Unique identifier for a list of orders submitted together as a basket or program trade.
notes: The buy-side assigns ListID when submitting a New Order List (MsgType=E). All orders in the list share the same ListID.

name: ListSeqNo
description: Sequence number of an order within its list, starting from 1.
notes: Used to identify the position of each individual order within a list basket. ListSeqNo=1 to TotNoOrders (68).

name: TotNoOrders
description: Total number of orders contained in the list.
notes: Required in New Order List. The receiving system expects ListSeqNo values from 1 to TotNoOrders.

name: AllocID
description: Unique identifier for an Allocation message. Assigned by the institution sending the Allocation Instruction; referenced as AllocID by the responding Allocation Instruction Ack. Must be unique across all allocation messages.
notes: Primary key for matching allocation messages to their acknowledgements. Referenced in Allocation Instruction Ack (P) to confirm or reject.

name: AllocTransType
description: Indicates the type of allocation transaction being submitted.

valid_values.1: Replace — replaces a previously sent allocation (RefAllocID required)
valid_values.2: Cancel — cancels a previously sent allocation (RefAllocID required)

notes: When using Replace (1) or Cancel (2), Tag 72 (RefAllocID) must contain the AllocID of the message being replaced or cancelled.

name: RefAllocID
description: Required when AllocTransType (71) is Replace (1) or Cancel (2). Contains the AllocID of the original Allocation Instruction being replaced or cancelled.
notes: Required when AllocTransType (71) = 1 (Replace) or 2 (Cancel). Points to the AllocID (70) being modified.

name: NoOrders
description: Number of orders being allocated in the repeating orders group within an Allocation Instruction.
notes: Header count for the repeating group of orders in an allocation. Each order includes ClOrdID (11), OrderID (37), etc.

name: AvgPrxPrecision
description: Number of decimal places to which average price should be rounded.
notes: Allows the institution to specify the precision of AvgPx (6) rounding for average price calculation across multiple fills.

name: TradeDate
description: Date of the trade in YYYYMMDD format. Represents the local market date, not the settlement date.
notes: The trade date in the local market timezone — the date when the exchange's trading session considered the trade to have occurred. May differ from the UTC date in SendingTime (52) for after-midnight sessions or when crossing midnight UTC. Not to be confused with settlement date.

name: ExecBroker
description: Identifier of the executing broker, typically a FINRA MPID or similar regulatory identifier.
notes: Deprecated in FIX 4.3 in favour of the Parties repeating group (NoPartyIDs / PartyID / PartyIDSource / PartyRole). Still widely seen in legacy FIX 4.2 implementations. Used for trade reporting to identify the executing broker's market participant ID.

name: PositionEffect
description: Indicates whether the resulting position after a trade should be treated as an opening or closing position.

valid_values.O: Open — establishing or adding to a position
valid_values.C: Close — reducing or closing an existing position
valid_values.F: FIFO — use first-in, first-out position accounting

notes: Critical for derivatives, futures, and options trading where position accounting affects margin requirements and regulatory reporting. In options trading, this determines whether the order opens a new position or closes an existing one, which can affect exercise rights and expiration handling.

name: NoAllocs
description: Number of allocation sub-accounts (repeating group). Each sub-account entry specifies the account, shares, and settlement instructions for one portion of the trade.
notes: Header count for the repeating group of individual sub-account allocations. Each sub-account has AllocAccount (79) and AllocShares (80).

name: AllocAccount
description: Identifies the sub-account within the allocation repeating group (Tag 78 NoAllocs). The format is counterparty-specific — typically a mnemonic representing a clearing account or fund.
notes: Each entry in the NoAllocs (78) repeating group specifies one allocation account. Format is agreed upon between parties.

name: AllocShares
description: Number of shares/contracts to allocate to the sub-account identified by AllocAccount (79) within the allocation repeating group.
notes: Must be specified per allocation sub-account. The sum of all AllocShares across the repeating group should equal the total trade quantity.

name: ProcessCode
description: Order processing code for the allocation, specifying commission and clearing arrangements.
notes: Used by clearing systems to route and process the allocation correctly based on the business arrangement.

name: AllocLinkID
description: Can be used to link two separate AllocationInstruction / ConfirmationRequest messages together. Both sides of a give-up trade set the same AllocLinkID to cross-reference the two allocations.

name: CxlQty
description: Total quantity cancelled on the order.
notes: Reflects the total cancelled quantity across all related orders in an allocation. Helps reconcile OrderQty vs. CumQty + CxlQty.

name: AllocStatus
description: Status of the allocation as reported by the sell-side in an Allocation Instruction Ack.
notes: In a Pending (3) status, a second Ack will follow when processing is complete.

name: AllocRejCode
description: Reason for rejection of an allocation.
notes: Populated when AllocStatus (87) is 1 (Block reject) or 2 (Account level reject).

name: PossResend
description: Indicates a message may be a retransmission of a previously sent application message.

valid_values.Y: Possible resend — check for duplicate application message

notes: Unlike PossDupFlag (43), PossResend applies to application messages rather than session-level retransmissions.

name: EncryptMethod
description: Method of encryption used for the session. In modern FIX implementations almost always 0 (None).
notes: Both sides must agree on EncryptMethod during Logon. Value 0 is required by most FIX networks and certification environments.

name: StopPx
description: The stop trigger price for a Stop or Stop Limit order.
notes: For Stop orders (OrdType=3), StopPx triggers conversion to a Market order. For Stop Limit (OrdType=4), triggers conversion to a Limit order at Price (44).

name: ExDestination
description: Execution destination — the specific exchange, ECN, or venue to which the order should be routed.
notes: Uses ISO 10383 MIC (Market Identifier Code). Used in smart order routing (SOR) to direct orders to specific venues. In Execution Reports, indicates where the fill actually occurred. When absent, the broker routes at its own discretion.

name: CxlRejReason
description: Coded reason why a cancel or cancel/replace request was rejected.

valid_values.3: Order already in Pending Cancel or Pending Replace
valid_values.4: Unable to process Order Mass Cancel Request
valid_values.5: OrigOrdModTime did not match last modification time

notes: Provides machine-readable reject reason alongside Text (58). Value 1 (Unknown order) is most common when the order no longer exists on the book.

name: OrdRejReason
description: Structured code identifying the reason an order was rejected.

valid_values.7: Duplicate of a Verbally Communicated Order

notes: Provides a machine-readable rejection reason alongside the human-readable Text (58) field. Automatically populated in Execution Reports where OrdStatus=Rejected (8) and in Order Cancel Rejects (MsgType=9). Systems should handle both the structured code and Text for complete rejection processing.

name: SecurityDesc
description: Free-form text description of the security.
notes: Not machine-parseable; for display/confirmation purposes. Often contains bond name, option series description, or fund name.

name: HeartBtInt
description: Heartbeat interval in seconds. Both parties must agree on this value during Logon.
notes: A Heartbeat (MsgType=0) is sent if no other message has been sent within HeartBtInt seconds. Typical values: 30 or 60 seconds. A test request is sent after two HeartBtInt periods with no received message.

name: ClientID
description: Firm identifier used in third-party transaction reporting, typically the end-client's identity.
notes: Deprecated in FIX 4.3 in favour of the Parties repeating group (NoPartyIDs/PartyID/PartyIDSource/PartyRole with PartyRole=3 for ClientID). Widely used in FIX 4.2 implementations to identify the end-client behind the order for regulatory reporting and allocations.

name: MinQty
description: Minimum quantity of an order that must be executed. If this quantity cannot be filled, the order (or remainder) is cancelled.
notes: Used when the client will not accept fills smaller than a specified threshold. Common in block trading contexts. Different from TimeInForce=FOK (fill-or-kill): FOK requires the entire OrderQty be filled, while MinQty allows partial fills above the minimum threshold.

name: MaxFloor
description: Maximum quantity of an order to be shown on the exchange book at any one time. Also known as the 'iceberg' or 'reserve' quantity.
notes: When specified, the exchange displays only MaxFloor shares at a time. After each fill, the displayed quantity replenishes from the reserve until the order is fully filled or cancelled. The total order size is hidden, reducing market impact for large orders. Not supported by all venues.

name: TestReqID
description: Unique identifier for a Test Request message, echoed back in the responding Heartbeat.
notes: The initiating side sends a Test Request with a unique TestReqID. The responding side sends a Heartbeat echoing the same TestReqID (112) to confirm the session is alive.

name: ReportToExch
description: Indicates which side is responsible for reporting the trade to the exchange.
notes: Relevant in markets where one party is required to report the transaction to the trade reporting facility.

name: LocateReqd
description: Indicates whether a locate is required before a short sale can be executed.

valid_values.Y: Locate required (order is a short sale)

notes: Applies to short sale orders (Side=5 or 6). Under Reg SHO, a locate must be obtained before short selling most equities.

name: OnBehalfOfCompID
description: Assigned value used to identify firm originating the message if the message was delivered by a third party. In give-up trading, the executing broker populates this with the give-up broker's CompID so the prime broker knows who executed the trade.
notes: Part of the standard optional FIX session header. Critical for give-up trades, prime-brokerage, and on-behalf-of workflows where messages are routed through or on behalf of intermediaries.

name: OnBehalfOfSubID
description: Assigned value used to identify the specific message originator within the firm identified by OnBehalfOfCompID (115). Supplements Tag 115 for fine-grained routing in intermediary scenarios.
notes: Part of the standard optional FIX session header. Critical for give-up trades, prime-brokerage, and on-behalf-of workflows where messages are routed through or on behalf of intermediaries.

name: QuoteID
description: Unique identifier for a quote, assigned by the quoting party (sell-side).
notes: When a buy-side wants to trade on a quote, they include the QuoteID in their New Order Single so the sell-side can associate the order with the original quote.

name: NetMoney
description: Total monetary value of the trade — price multiplied by quantity.
notes: Net of commissions and accrued interest. Currency specified by Currency (15).

name: SettlCurrAmt
description: Total amount due in settlement currency.
notes: The settlement amount after FX conversion. Used with SettlCurrency (120) and SettlCurrFxRate (155).

name: SettlCurrency
description: Currency in which settlement will occur.
notes: ISO 4217 three-character code. When different from Currency (15), SettlCurrFxRate (155) gives the conversion rate.

name: OrigSendingTime
description: Original SendingTime when a message is retransmitted during gap-fill.
notes: Required when PossDupFlag (43)=Y. Preserves the original timestamp so the receiver can identify the original message in logs.

name: GapFillFlag
description: Distinguishes a gap-fill Sequence Reset from a hard reset to sequence 1.

valid_values.Y: Gap fill — skip messages in the resend range

notes: When GapFillFlag=Y, the Sequence Reset message is skipping over messages that don't need retransmission (e.g. Heartbeats). NewSeqNo (36) is the next expected sequence number.

name: ExpireTime
description: Date and time the order expires if not fully filled.
notes: Used with TimeInForce (59)=6 (Good Till Date/Time). Must be in UTC. See also ExpireDate (432) for date-only precision.

name: DeliverToCompID
description: Assigned value used to identify the firm targeted to receive the message when delivered via a third-party intermediary. Used in prime-brokerage and give-up workflows to forward confirmations and allocations.
notes: Part of the standard optional FIX session header. Critical for give-up trades, prime-brokerage, and on-behalf-of workflows where messages are routed through or on behalf of intermediaries.

name: DeliverToSubID
description: Assigned value used to identify a specific individual or unit within the target firm for message delivery via a third party. Supplements DeliverToCompID (128) for intra-firm routing.
notes: Part of the standard optional FIX session header. Critical for give-up trades, prime-brokerage, and on-behalf-of workflows where messages are routed through or on behalf of intermediaries.

name: QuoteReqID
description: Unique identifier for a Quote Request, echoed in the Quote response.
notes: The QuoteReqID links a Quote (S) response back to the originating Quote Request (R). The quoting party echoes it in the Quote.

name: BidPx
description: Best bid price offered by the quoting party.
notes: The price at which the quoting party is willing to buy. Used in two-sided quotes alongside BidSize (134) and OfferPx (133).

name: OfferPx
description: Best offer (ask) price offered by the quoting party.
notes: The price at which the quoting party is willing to sell. Used alongside OfferSize (135) and BidPx (132).

name: BidSize
description: Quantity available at the bid price.
notes: The maximum quantity the quoting party is willing to buy at BidPx (132).

name: OfferSize
description: Quantity available at the offer price.
notes: The maximum quantity the quoting party is willing to sell at OfferPx (133).

name: PrevClosePx
description: Previous closing price for the security, used as a reference or benchmark.
notes: Used for informational purposes or to calculate peg offsets. Not a trading instruction.

name: ResetSeqNumFlag
description: Signals that both parties should reset their sequence numbers to 1.
notes: Included in a Logon (A) to perform a clean session start. Both sides must agree — if one side sends Y and the other sends N, the session typically fails. Common at business day open.

name: SenderLocationID
description: Optional geographic location of the sender (e.g. city code or trading desk location).
notes: Supplements SenderCompID (49). Used by some regulatory regimes to identify the physical location of the order originator.

name: TargetLocationID
description: Optional geographic location of the target.
notes: Supplements TargetCompID (56). Rarely used in practice but available for routing in multi-location networks.

name: ExecType
description: Describes what happened in this specific Execution Report — the type of transaction being reported.
notes: Added in FIX 4.2 to replace the older ExecTransType (20). ExecType=F (Trade) is the most important: it signals that a fill occurred. Always read ExecType and OrdStatus (39) together — ExecType tells you what just happened; OrdStatus tells you the cumulative order state. ExecType=D (Restated) is used by venues to push unsolicited corrections.

name: LeavesQty
description: Quantity of the order remaining open for further execution. LeavesQty = OrderQty − CumQty.
notes: Added in FIX 4.2 alongside ExecType (150). When LeavesQty reaches 0, the order is complete (either fully filled or cancelled). For cancelled and rejected orders, LeavesQty is set to 0 even though CumQty < OrderQty. A non-zero LeavesQty with OrdStatus=Filled indicates a data error.

name: CashOrderQty
description: Order quantity expressed in cash (monetary) terms rather than number of shares or contracts.
notes: Mutual-fund and retail orders often specify a cash amount rather than a share quantity. The venue converts to shares at the execution price.

name: SettlCurrFxRate
description: FX conversion rate used to convert from the trade currency (Currency 15) to settlement currency (SettlCurrency 120).
notes: Applies when SettlCurrency (120) differs from Currency (15). SettlCurrAmt (119) = NetMoney (118) × SettlCurrFxRate (155).

name: AccruedInterestRate
description: Accrued interest rate as a percentage of par value, for fixed-income securities.
notes: Used in bond trades. Combined with AccruedInterestAmt (159) to determine the total settlement amount.

name: AccruedInterestAmt
description: Accrued interest amount in currency for fixed-income securities.
notes: Dirty price = clean price + AccruedInterestAmt. Important for bond settlement and allocation calculations.

name: SecurityType
description: Indicates the asset class or type of security being traded.
notes: Determines which fields are relevant and which message types are applicable. Different asset classes have materially different required fields — for example, FX orders typically omit Price (44) and use dedicated FX tags instead. Also important for regulatory routing and reporting decisions.

name: EffectiveTime
description: Date and time the order becomes effective (future-dated orders).
notes: Allows scheduling an order to activate at a future time. Exchange must support time-triggered order entry.

name: AllocLinkID
description: Identifier linking multiple allocation messages to a single FX trade for netting or swap purposes.
notes: Used with AllocLinkType (197) for FX netting or swap structures where multiple allocations relate to one trade.

name: AllocLinkType
description: Type of link represented by AllocLinkID (196).
notes: Indicates how related allocation messages should be processed together — as netted FX positions or as a swap transaction.

name: SecondaryOrderID
description: Order identifier assigned by the broker, secondary to OrderID (37) assigned by the exchange.
notes: Allows the broker to carry its own internal order reference separate from the venue's OrderID. Useful for drop-copy and reconciliation.

name: MaturityMonthYear
description: Month and year of maturity or expiration for derivatives, in YYYYMM format.
notes: Required for futures and options to identify the contract series. Combined with MaturityDay (205) for exact expiry date. Superseded by MaturityDate (541) in FIX 4.4.

name: PutOrCall
description: Indicates whether an option is a put or a call.
notes: Required for options orders. Must match the terms of the option contract being traded.

name: StrikePrice
description: Strike price of the option.
notes: For options, identifies the specific contract along with Symbol (55), MaturityMonthYear (200), and PutOrCall (201).

name: CoveredOrUncovered
description: Indicates whether an options position is covered or uncovered.
notes: Covered options require existing position in the underlying; uncovered options have higher margin requirements and regulatory implications.

name: OptAttribute
description: Additional attribute to differentiate option series — typically a letter suffix.
notes: Used when multiple option series exist for the same strike, maturity, and put/call, differentiated by an additional character. Exchange-specific.

name: MaturityDay
description: Specific calendar day within MaturityMonthYear for the expiry date.
notes: Value is 1-31. Superseded by MaturityDate (541) in FIX 4.4 which provides the full YYYYMMDD date.

name: SecurityExchange
description: Primary exchange where the security is listed.
notes: ISO 10383 MIC code. Helps identify the security when Symbol (55) alone is ambiguous (e.g. dual-listed securities).

name: PegDifference
description: Offset from the peg price for pegged orders. Renamed PegOffsetValue in FIX 4.4.
notes: For ExecInst (18) containing pegged instructions. The signed offset from the reference price (e.g. -0.01 for 1 cent below midpoint).

name: CouponRate
description: Coupon interest rate for a fixed-income security, expressed as a percentage.
notes: Used to identify a specific bond series alongside Issuer, MaturityDate, and other instrument identifiers.

name: CouponPaymentDate
description: Date of the next coupon payment in YYYYMMDD format.
notes: Used in bond and fixed-income order identification.

name: IssueDate
description: Original issuance date of the security in YYYYMMDD format.
notes: Used alongside other bond identifiers to unambiguously identify a fixed-income instrument.

name: Factor
description: Amortization factor for mortgage-backed securities, expressed as a decimal fraction of original par.
notes: For MBS and ABS, Factor adjusts the face value for principal paydowns. Used to calculate settlement amount from par value.

name: RefTagID
description: Tag number of the field that caused a session-level Reject.
notes: When a session-level Reject (3) is sent, RefTagID indicates which field in the rejected message caused the problem.

name: RefMsgType
description: MsgType (35) value of the rejected message.
notes: In a session-level Reject (3) or Business Message Reject (j), identifies the type of message that was rejected.

name: SessionRejectReason
description: Coded reason for a session-level Reject.

valid_values.2: Tag not defined for message type
valid_values.5: Value out of range for this tag
valid_values.14: Tag specified out of required order
valid_values.15: Repeating group fields out of order

notes: Used with RefTagID (371) to pinpoint the exact nature of a malformed or invalid message.

name: DKReason
description: Reason for a Don't Know Trade (DK) response. Indicates why the counterparty is unable to confirm or match the trade being referenced.
notes: Required in Don't Know Trade (Q) messages. "7 - Other" should be accompanied by a free-text explanation in Tag 58 (Text).

name: BusinessRejectRefID
description: The value of the identifier field from the message that was rejected at the business level.
notes: Contains the value from the ID field of the rejected message (e.g. the ClOrdID for a rejected New Order Single).

name: BusinessRejectReason
description: Coded reason for a Business Message Reject.
notes: Used alongside Text (58) to provide machine-readable business rejection reason.

name: GrossTradeAmt
description: Total trade value before commissions, fees, and accrued interest.
notes: Price × Quantity. NetMoney (118) = GrossTradeAmt minus commissions.

name: NoContraBrokers
description: Number of contra broker entries in the repeating group.
notes: Header for the contra broker repeating group in an Execution Report. Used for average price systems and EMS reporting.

name: DiscretionInst
description: Defines the reference price from which discretionary instruction applies.
notes: Used with DiscretionOffsetValue (389) to define a discretionary price range around a reference point.

name: DiscretionOffsetValue
description: Signed offset from the DiscretionInst reference price forming the discretionary price limit.
notes: Negative values indicate below the peg; positive values above. E.g. DiscretionInst=4 (midpoint), DiscretionOffsetValue=-0.02 means discretion to 2 cents below mid.

name: PriceType
description: Describes how the Price (44) value should be interpreted.
notes: Critical for fixed-income where price is often expressed as percentage of par or yield rather than a cash price.

name: DayOrderQty
description: Order quantity for the current trading day on a multi-day GTC order.
notes: For GTC orders, reflects how much of the order was active today specifically. Separate from the cumulative total over all days.

name: DayCumQty
description: Cumulative filled quantity for the current trading day on a multi-day GTC order.
notes: Today's filled quantity on a GTC order. Resets to 0 each trading day while CumQty (14) accumulates across all days.

name: DayAvgPx
description: Average fill price for the current trading day on a multi-day GTC order.
notes: Today's average price, distinct from AvgPx (6) which is the all-days average. Both are populated for GTC orders.

name: GTBookingInst
description: Specifies booking instruction for GTC orders at end of trading day.

valid_values.0: Book out all trades on day of execution
valid_values.1: Accumulate until order is completed or expires

notes: Relevant only for GTC orders. Determines whether each day's fills are booked out separately or accumulated.

name: ExpireDate
description: Date the order expires, in YYYYMMDD (local market date) format.
notes: Required when TimeInForce (59)=6 (Good Till Date). More common than ExpireTime (126) for date-only expiry. US equities GTD orders typically use this field.

name: CxlRejResponseTo
description: Identifies whether the Order Cancel Reject is responding to a Cancel or a Cancel/Replace request.
notes: Required in FIX 4.4+. Clarifies which type of modify request triggered this rejection.

name: ClearingAccount
description: Clearing account number for the order.
notes: Used when the clearing account differs from the trading account (Account 1). Required by some clearing firms.

name: PartyIDSource
description: Identifies the naming convention used for the PartyID (448) value.
notes: Part of the repeating Parties group (NoPartyIDs/PartyID/PartyIDSource/PartyRole). Always accompanies PartyID (448).

name: PartyID
description: Identifier for a party in the transaction, such as a broker, clearing firm, or client.
notes: Part of the NoPartyIDs (453) repeating group. The format depends on PartyIDSource (447). PartyRole (452) specifies the party's role.

name: PartyRole
description: Role of the party identified in PartyID (448).
notes: Each entry in the repeating parties group has a distinct role. Replaces deprecated fields ExecBroker (76) and ClientID (109).

name: NoPartyIDs
description: Number of entries in the Parties repeating group.
notes: Header count for the parties group. Each entry provides PartyID (448), PartyIDSource (447), and PartyRole (452). Replaces the deprecated ExecBroker (76) and ClientID (109) fields.

name: NoSecurityAltID
description: Number of alternate security identifiers in the repeating group.
notes: Header for the security alternate ID repeating group. Allows a security to be identified by multiple schemes simultaneously.

name: SecurityAltID
description: Alternate security identifier value.
notes: Used in the NoSecurityAltID repeating group to provide additional security identifiers (e.g. both CUSIP and ISIN for the same security).

name: SecurityAltIDSource
description: Source of the alternate security identifier in SecurityAltID (455).
notes: Same value set as SecurityIDSource (22). Always accompanies SecurityAltID (455) in the repeating group.

name: Product
description: High-level product classification for the instrument.
notes: Broad classification used for routing, reporting, and margin calculations.

name: CFICode
description: ISO 10962 Classification of Financial Instruments (CFI) code — 6-character alphanumeric.
notes: Standardized 6-character code classifying the instrument type. First character indicates category (E=Equity, D=Debt, F=Future, O=Option, etc.).

name: CountryOfIssue
description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the country where the security was issued.
notes: Used for instrument identification, particularly for bonds. E.g. 'US', 'GB', 'DE'.

name: StateOrProvinceOfIssue
description: State or province where the municipal or local-authority security was issued.
notes: Used primarily for US municipal bonds to identify the issuing state (e.g. 'NY', 'CA').

name: LocaleOfIssue
description: Locale (city, county, or district) where the municipal security was issued.
notes: Used for US municipal bonds alongside StateOrProvinceOfIssue (471).

name: TransBkdTime
description: Time the trade was booked at the exchange or matching engine.
notes: Captures the exchange-side booking time for the trade. Used in T+1 reconciliation and audit trail.

name: TradeReportTransType
description: Transaction type of a Trade Capture Report.

valid_values.5: Cancel Due To Back Out Of Trade

notes: Indicates whether the Trade Capture Report is a new trade, a correction, or a cancellation.

name: MaturityDate
description: Final maturity date of the instrument in YYYYMMDD format.
notes: Supersedes MaturityMonthYear (200) and MaturityDay (205) in FIX 4.4+. Provides the exact expiry date for derivatives or maturity for bonds.

name: NoSides
description: Number of sides in a cross order repeating group.
notes: Required in New Order Cross (MsgType=s). Always 2 for a cross — one buy side and one sell side.

name: Username
description: Logon username. Used in FIX Logon (A) messages to authenticate the session initiator. Typically provided by the broker or exchange during connectivity setup.
notes: Added in FIX 4.3. When used, typically required alongside Tag 554 (Password). The exact authentication mechanism is counterparty-specific.

name: Password
description: Logon password. Used alongside Tag 553 (Username) in FIX Logon (A) messages for session authentication. Transmitted in cleartext in the FIX message; production environments typically use encrypted sessions or one-time passwords.
notes: Added in FIX 4.3. Counterparties that require authentication via Tag 98 EncryptMethod may use this field. Treat with care — the value appears in FIX logs.

name: TradeReportID
description: Unique identifier for a Trade Capture Report, assigned by the reporting party.
notes: Primary key for trade capture reporting. Referenced in subsequent corrections or cancellations via TradeReportRefID (572).

name: TradeReportRefID
description: Reference to a prior Trade Capture Report being corrected or cancelled.
notes: Contains the TradeReportID (571) of the original report when TradeReportTransType (487) is Cancel (1) or Replace (2).

name: ClOrdLinkID
description: Links a new order to a previously transmitted order for algorithmic strategy chains.
notes: Used to associate orders in an algorithmic strategy chain (e.g. bracket orders, contingent orders).

name: MassStatusReqID
description: Unique identifier for an Order Mass Status Request.
notes: The sell-side echoes MassStatusReqID in each Execution Report sent in response to the mass status request.

name: MassStatusReqType
description: Specifies the scope of orders to be included in the mass status response.

valid_values.1: Status for orders for a security
valid_values.2: Status for orders for an underlying security
valid_values.3: Status for orders for a product
valid_values.4: Status for orders for a CFICode
valid_values.5: Status for orders for a SecurityType
valid_values.6: Status for orders for a trading session
valid_values.8: Status for orders for a PartyID

notes: Allows buy-side to request status for a broad set of orders in a single message.

name: StartDate
description: Start date for a swap or time-bounded order in YYYYMMDD format.
notes: Used for FX swaps, repo agreements, and other instruments with a defined start and end date.

name: EndDate
description: End date for a swap or time-bounded order in YYYYMMDD format.
notes: The far leg date for FX swaps or term repos. Used with StartDate (588).

name: LegSide
description: Side (buy or sell) for an individual leg in a multileg order.
notes: Each leg in a multileg order has its own LegSide within the repeating legs group.

name: AllocType
description: Describes the specific type or purpose of an Allocation message. Determines how the receiving party should interpret and process the allocation instruction.

valid_values.1: Calculated — calculated quantities reported back to the executing broker
valid_values.3: Sellside Calculated Using Preliminary (with MiscFees and NetMoney added)
valid_values.4: Sellside Calculated without Preliminary (sent unsolicited from the sellside)
valid_values.6: Buyside Ready-to-Book — Combined Set of Orders

notes: Required in Allocation Instruction (J). Determines processing path — type 1 (Calculated) is the most common in post-trade confirmation flows.

name: LongQty
description: Long position quantity in a position report.
notes: Reports the total long quantity held in the position.

name: ShortQty
description: Short position quantity in a position report.
notes: Reports the total short quantity held in the position.

name: ClearingBusinessDate
description: The business date used for clearing the trade.
notes: May differ from TradeDate (75) when the trade occurs after clearing cutoff time.

name: PosReqID
description: Unique identifier for a position request or report.
notes: Assigned by the initiating party when requesting position data. Echoed in the Position Report response.

name: PosReqType
description: Specifies the type of position data being requested.
notes: Determines the content of the Position Report response.

name: PosReqResult
description: Result of the position request.
notes: First field to check in a Position Report to determine whether the request succeeded.

name: SettlPrice
description: Settlement price used for mark-to-market or final settlement.
notes: Used in derivatives position reports. The type of settlement price is identified by SettlPriceType (731).

name: SettlPriceType
description: Type of settlement price in SettlPrice (730).
notes: Final = actual exchange-published settlement. Theoretical = estimated value before official settlement.

name: NextExpectedMsgSeqNum
description: Indicates the next sequence number the sender expects to receive from the counterparty, enabling gap detection and orderly session recovery.
notes: When present in a Logon, the counterparty should verify no sequence gaps exist and resend any missed messages if needed.

name: OrderCategory
description: Categorizes an order for trade reporting and capacity purposes.
notes: Used for post-trade reporting and capacity monitoring under MiFID II and other regulatory frameworks.

name: ApplVerID
description: Identifies the FIX application version (message body version) when using FIXT 1.1 transport.
notes: In FIX 5.0 / FIXT 1.1, the session layer is separated from the application layer. ApplVerID specifies which FIX version governs the message body.

name: CstmApplVerID
description: Custom application version identifier for proprietary extensions beyond the standard FIX spec.
notes: Used alongside ApplVerID (1128) when a firm has defined extensions to the standard FIX application version.

name: DefaultApplVerID
description: The default application version for all messages in this session, specified during Logon.
notes: Required in the Logon (A) message when using FIXT 1.1 to declare the default FIX application version for the session. Individual messages may override with ApplVerID (1128).
